extracted_items.content: User is building a REST API backend for a task management app called 'taskflow-api'
Technology stack decision: FastAPI (web framework), PostgreSQL (database), SQLAlchemy (ORM), Alembic (migrations), Pydantic v2 (data validation)
User prefers uv for package management over pip or poetry due to speed (Rust-based resolver)

Project structure: app/ (with main.py, models/, schemas/, routers/, services/, db.py), alembic/ for migrations, tests/ directory, pyproject.toml. Business logic kept in services, not in route handlers
Testing stack: pytest with pytest-asyncio for async tests and httpx for FastAPI endpoint testing. Minimum 80% test coverage required

Deployment and CI/CD: Docker (multi-stage build recommended), Fly.io for hosting, GitHub Actions for CI pipeline. CI pipeline runs: ruff linting, pytest with coverage check, Docker image build on main branch
User chose Pydantic v2 for breaking changes trade-off: accepts validator migration burden (@validator → @field_validator) for significant performance improvements

scenarios.title: Scenario 2: Heavy Dedup (Many Existing Memories Overlap)

extracted_items.content: Background extraction model is claude-haiku-4-5 (Haiku 4.5) for improved instruction following and reduced hallucination

deduped_items.content: Background extraction model is claude-haiku-4-5 (Haiku 4.5) for improved instruction following and reduced hallucination

extracted_items.supersedes_content: comfygit project is deployed on Heroku
extracted_items.content: comfygit project is deployed on Fly.io (migrated from Heroku last week). Primary region is iad (US East). Possible future expansion to sin (Singapore) for Asia coverage but not yet decided.

extracted_items.supersedes_content: comfygit API is currently on v2
extracted_items.content: comfygit API is on v3 (v2 fully deprecated as of last month). v2 endpoints return 410 Gone response. Migration guide at docs.comfygit.org/migration/v3. Key breaking changes: authentication moved to OAuth2 PKCE; workflow endpoint renamed from /workflows to /environments.

extracted_items.supersedes_content: comfygit uses Redis for job queue processing
extracted_items.content: comfygit uses PostgreSQL-backed job queuing via pgqueue Python library (replaced Redis). Processing ~500 jobs/day. Redis was retired because it was overkill for the current scale.
comfygit database is Neon (serverless PostgreSQL, migrated from AWS RDS). Chosen for cost efficiency with variable workload pattern.
comfygit workflow endpoint is /environments (renamed from /workflows in API v3).

extracted_items.content: Token refresh race condition in React Native app: when app resumes from background, multiple requests simultaneously attempt token refresh. Fix: implement promise-based mutex to ensure only one refresh is in-flight at a time; subsequent requests return the same promise.
Token storage: migrate from AsyncStorage to expo-secure-store for encrypted token storage. AsyncStorage is unencrypted and vulnerable if device is rooted.
Minimum supported OS versions: iOS 16 (minimum), Android API 29 (Android 10 minimum). These are fixed requirements.

deduped_items.content: Token refresh race condition in React Native app: when app resumes from background, multiple requests simultaneously attempt token refresh. Fix: implement promise-based mutex to ensure only one refresh is in-flight at a time; subsequent requests return the same promise.
Token storage: migrate from AsyncStorage to expo-secure-store for encrypted token storage. AsyncStorage is unencrypted and vulnerable if device is rooted.
Minimum supported OS versions: iOS 16 (minimum), Android API 29 (Android 10 minimum). These are fixed requirements.

extracted_items.content: JWT tokens for this project use RS256 (asymmetric) signing, not HS256, for production security. Public key is distributed for verification without exposing the private key.
Auth system uses stateless JWT authentication — tokens are verified by signature without server-side session storage.
Auth configuration: sessions last 24 hours, refresh tokens last 30 days, MFA is required, rate limiting is 60 requests/minute, AUTH_PROVIDER is 'internal'.

deduped_items.content: JWT tokens for this project use RS256 (asymmetric) signing, not HS256, for production security. Public key is distributed for verification without exposing the private key.
Auth system uses stateless JWT authentication — tokens are verified by signature without server-side session storage.
Auth configuration: sessions last 24 hours, refresh tokens last 30 days, MFA is required, rate limiting is 60 requests/minute, AUTH_PROVIDER is 'internal'.

extracted_items.content: Analytics service database: ClickHouse (columnar storage, purpose-built for analytics). PostgreSQL was considered but rejected in favor of ClickHouse for better aggregation performance on analytics workloads.
Event streaming for analytics pipeline: Redpanda (Kafka-compatible, single binary, no Zookeeper dependency). Selected over Apache Kafka for operational simplicity.
Analytics dashboard: Grafana, using the Grafana ClickHouse plugin as data source. Leveraging existing Grafana infrastructure for infrastructure metrics.

Analytics stack architecture: Redpanda (event streaming) → ClickHouse (columnar analytics storage) → Grafana (dashboard/visualization). Designed for a few thousand queries per day with complex aggregations.

deduped_items.content: Analytics service database: ClickHouse (columnar storage, purpose-built for analytics). PostgreSQL was considered but rejected in favor of ClickHouse for better aggregation performance on analytics workloads.
Event streaming for analytics pipeline: Redpanda (Kafka-compatible, single binary, no Zookeeper dependency). Selected over Apache Kafka for operational simplicity.
Analytics dashboard: Grafana, using the Grafana ClickHouse plugin as data source. Leveraging existing Grafana infrastructure for infrastructure metrics.

Analytics stack architecture: Redpanda (event streaming) → ClickHouse (columnar analytics storage) → Grafana (dashboard/visualization). Designed for a few thousand queries per day with complex aggregations.

extracted_items.content: CI/CD pipeline uses GitHub Actions with the following stages: lint → test → build docker image → push to GHCR → deploy to Fly.io. Deployment to Fly.io only occurs on main branch.
Test matrix configuration: Python 3.11 and 3.12 must both be supported and validated by CI pipeline.
Frontend stack: Next.js 14 with App Router, TypeScript required for all files (no plain JavaScript allowed).
UI component library: shadcn/ui with Tailwind CSS. MUI and Chakra explicitly not used.
API rate limiting: per-user tiered limits of 100 requests per minute for free tier and 1000 requests per minute for paid tier.
Rate limit implementation: Redis with sliding window algorithm using ZSET, leveraging existing Redis job queue infrastructure.
Feature flag naming convention: {team}_{feature_name}_{state} where state is one of: enabled, disabled, rollout. Example: payments_new_checkout_enabled. Enforced in LaunchDarkly.
Feature flag management platform: LaunchDarkly.

scenarios.title: Scenario 8: Very Short Window / Pure Greetings (Edge Case)

extracted_items.content: Architecture: modular monolith. Extract services only when there is a clear scaling need.
API design: GraphQL with Apollo Server for multi-client support (web, mobile, third-party integrations).
Authentication: OAuth2 with PKCE for all clients. JWT tokens using RS256 algorithm. No API key authentication for first-party clients.
Search infrastructure: Elasticsearch for complex full-text search requirements.
Caching: Redis for session cache and hot data. TTL: 1 hour default, 24 hours for user profiles.
Background jobs: Celery with Redis broker. Periodic tasks via Celery Beat. No separate job service.
File storage: S3-compatible via Cloudflare R2. CDN via Cloudflare. AWS S3 rejected due to egress costs.
Observability: structured JSON logs via Grafana Loki, traces via OpenTelemetry → Grafana Tempo. ELK stack rejected as too heavy.
Infrastructure as Code: Terraform for all cloud resources. Manual console changes strictly forbidden — everything must be version-controlled in IaC.
Secrets management: HashiCorp Vault for all secrets. No secrets in environment variables directly. Non-negotiable policy.
Error tracking: Sentry integrated across all services.
Code review policy: PRs require 2 approvals minimum. No self-merge on main branch. Main branch protected. Feature flags required for all risky deploys.

extracted_items.content: Primary GitHub organization is github.com/acme-corp. All project repositories are hosted under this organization.
Primary programming language across the organization is Python. TypeScript is used for frontend only.
Staging environment is at staging.acme-corp.com with a separate database. Production environment is at acme-corp.com. Database environments must never be mixed or cross-contaminated.
On-call rotation operates on a 2-week cycle. Alert notifications are posted to the #platform-alerts Slack channel.
Always run the test suite after making code changes without being prompted. Do not skip tests.
Use conventional commits format for all commit messages: type(scope): message. Types are feat/fix/chore/docs/refactor/test. Always include a scope in parentheses.
Provide short, direct responses. Use bullet points only when there are 3 or more items. Do not include preamble.
When asked to review code, check for issues in this order: security issues first, then correctness, then style.
